text: Yeah, finally saw it. Disappointing, which I say as a huge fan of the first two. Won't go as far to say it's horrible or anything like what some reviewers and critics are saying -- but I CAN empathize with those saying that esp. if they are big fans of the first two films.  This movie does have a lot of potential: well-acted, a great plot twist, some wonderful scenes showing everyone's abilities...the first 50-75% of the film is great, actually, but the ending devastates it (plus the promise of a battle that never happens, was a Chekhov Gun in this script).

text:
Imo, the movie has potential to be an amazing sequel, but it turns out to be another filler that you has less impact than the previous movies. Let's be honest, it can be way way way more amazing if Shyamalan combine the cinematography of "Unbreakable" and the script of "Split", yet we see little to none of these great points in the movie. Acting is....well...acceptable i guess. The only ones believable in this movie are the "Split" casts as they were in a great movie before and also they are good actors/actresses ( James Mcavoy is pretty much carrying the whole sequel). Samuel's death is kinda off since i expect for a brainer psychopath he could have been out-smart everyone till the end, but i guess that's what you need to end a trilogy.


Tl, DR: it's another Micheal Bay's Transformer sequel. Fun to watch, fun to see old characters meet up, but don't really care about plot.

text:
The movie was wonderful.
If you liked the movies "Unbreakable" and "split", you will enjoy this movie. Also seeing "Unbreakable" and "split" first is a must. The movie studios keep giving us remakes or the same basic story over and over again, but not with these movies. These movies are fresh and original. It's not your typical dime-a-dozen superhero movie, nor a fast pace mindless action film. Glass is clever and completely well done. It takes a necessary time to build the story and characters.

The second half was ass, just too messy and somewhat unbalanced. Bruce Willis had little to no screentime. this felt more like a Split sequel than a finale. too many pov shots, there were times during the 3rd act where i said, ''this is too goofy now with the whole comic book stuff''. Samuel Jackson didnt do shit till the 3rd act as well which was kinda wack. i know it was a low-bugdet film, but i was really looking forward to another climatic fight scene but in the tower downtown.  the ending was just straight up stupid asf, THREE TWISTS MY NIGGA HUH?! (5/10)

text:
DON'T LISTEN TO THE CRITICS! M. Night has done it again! I was at the edge of my seat trying to figure out how this will end and then when it does my jaw drops, which is is a common recurring thing that happens when I watch M. Night's movies and that is very well a GOOD thing, which means he is doing something right!
This movie is not like any other superhero movie I have seen. M. Night brings such a realistic bend. From the fight scenes, which are played out like it truly would be in real life, the conflict through the characters of battling the belief in real super powers, to the whole demeanor of the movie, it has such a realistic and literal vibe to the plot that sets it apart from all the other superhero movies and making the audience truly believe that the concept of superpowers could actually be real. Its stunningly executed from the cinematography, score, color scheme, plot and all of the above. Not to Mention James McAvoy's acting was presented stunningly. I didn't think his acting could get any better after Split, but it did! WHY HASN'T THIS GUY WON AN OSCAR YET!?!?
If your looking for a remarkable superhero movie like no other, I wouldn't recommend any other movie than this one!

text: Man I really liked Unbreakable when I first saw it years ago and it's more "realistic" as to what a super hero and villain would look like in the real world if it weren't fantasy.  I love this kind of atmosphere compared to the repetitive explosions, hi-tech, out of this world abilities and cheesy pick up lines.  There's just something about it being so much more normal which makes you feel like there are really people that have powers that nobody can ever explain.  It's like all those magazines and new stories of a person having the strength to turn over a burning vehicle to save someone's life or someone that ran into a flaming building to somehow make it alive and save everyone... maybe there really are super heroes in the world, cuz there are certainly a lot of villains...

text:
Prediction: Casey from Split will emerge with mind control/suggestive powers. 

1. The broken are the more evolved. She is confirmed as broken, which points to her being a candidate to be more evolved.

2. She somehow had the attention of all the personalities, and could seemingly always get them to cater to her and even admire her. Almost as if the Horde sensed something different about her. Think about it, every personality treated her differently than the other girls, Patricia and Hedwig in particular, and the simple fact the Beast chose not to kill her was an obvious clue that there was something more to her.

3. She told the girl to pee, and the girl did. Now, this sounds like suggestive reasoning, and maybe it is, but is it possible that she implanted that into the girls mind, notice how she grabs her head......which I'll speak further on later.

4. Now this is stretching a bit, but did you notice that when Kevin first gets in the car with the girls, that he seemingly didn't even notice Casey was right there next to him? Almost as if she had somehow concealed his mind from seeing her when she got scared, until her concentration broke when she opened the door and all the sudden he saw her...notice in particular how he looks at her as if he was surprised. The Dennis personality is OCD and extremely detailed in his approach. how did he not notice her right away or make a point to go after her right away being she was the closest? 

5. At the end with the cop, the look she gave the cop that signaled the cop to something being wrong. Now...pay special attention to the cops face, it’s not just the cop getting something is wrong. It’s an almost shocked look as if the cop can see what she’s thinking and reacting to it.

Now back to the grabbing the girls head part, watch this trailer and see how she once again grabs Kevin by the head and how he reacts, he looks shocked, or stunned even. Which indicates she may be exhibiting her mind control ability.

COMMON THEMES BETWEEN UNBREAKABLE AND SPLIT:

 Glass is weak and genius and malevolent and the opposite to David Dunn’s durability and simple focus, and benevolence. 

Kevin is split and unable to control even his own mind and primal, while Casey is whole and able to control other minds and nurturing.

Also keep in mind, Mr. Glass in fact 'awakened' David Dunn to what he could really do and what he really was, by finding his opposite on the spectrum. The 'broken are the more evolved' line, I believe, is the moment when Casey was also awakened to being more evolved. Regardless of whether she initially realized it or not or even knew what that meant. Continuing the theme of the awakened heroes via the villains.......

I keep coming back to this trailer and the only thing that still bugs me is how nonchalantly Kevin Crumb/The Horde is revealed to be in the story. With all that build up of the Psychiatrist talking, imagine her head obscuring the middle section of the room, showing only Elijah and David, then cut to Davids face with a laugh coming off screen, he turns and then you see that side profile shot of Dennis clapping and saying "Good for you", revealing the horde was in the middle. Just that little bit of extra build up before dropping it instead of just showing him sitting there. 

Ultimately it doesn't matter, but it's just a small detail that I keep thinking about.

text: This should be Shyamalan's slate for the next ten years: building his own unique superhero universe. He should also try to bring onboard other projects, maybe those that didn't work out as well, and make them apart of the mythology. Lady in the Water has potential: not nymphs or fantasy creatures but something else entirely. Could be interesting. Also, if not that, then find a way to bring in Bryce Dallas Howard into the universe and give her a superhero background. He should consult with other filmmakers too, maybe, and pull more Split twist endings. Honestly, this could be really exceptional and add a layer to the superhero genre which, I think we can agree, could always use more layering.

text:
After being rescued, Casey will receive medical treatment, and there authorities will find out the scars on her belly so they will lock up her abusive uncle.
Price's sweet mouth will persuade both The Beast and Dunn to kill each other as he would love to see the greatest showdown on  earth. He's like a promotor who wants to arrange a fight. His hair reminds me of Don King.
The Beast would love to kill Dunn because Dunn is impure and has no scar on his body. The Beast won't kill Price because Price has so many scars on his body.
Dunn maybe as strong as the Beast but not as agile. The Beast can jump higher and can climb walls. He will defeat Dunn but he can't kill him because Dunn is the luckiest man on earth. All those punches and kicks and bites (etcetera) that he receives from the Beast won't leave a mark.
Though Price can manipulate the Beast's mind, he won't find it easy to get into both Dennis' and Patricia's. Since Dennis can pretend to be Barry, he can also pretend to be the Beast to fool Price.
Crumb's personality will come up after being called by Casey. He will regret everything and tried to kill himself. But that won't be easy because other personalities will resist him.
Well those are just my theory. You all may disagree with me. 😃

text:
I never thought Unbreakable or any of Shyamalan's films to be the type that needed sequels. And I always liked how he was always coming up with original stories that he wrote and directed himself. I also didn't really think of Unbreakable as a superhero film. Just a drama about someone who is LIKE a superhero. 

But I have to say, that I am super hyped about this. It makes sense to make a sequel to Unbreakable because that was just him discovering what he is and now we can finally see the real action take place. I still haven't seen Split but I'm dying to. These movies look really good. This is the perfect time for something like this to come out with all these DC and Marvel movies that are flooding in.

text:
“Because of the kids...” -Mr. Glass 

Shout out to everybody that’s only interested in this because of Split. And THANK YOU 🙏! Jules Winnfield is a BMF and all; but Mr. Glass will always be my favorite Samuel L. Jackson character. Unbreakable was released when I was 18, and I was blown away. The critics weren’t. I thought I’d never see Mr. Glass again. I only watched Split, because someone 10-years younger than me; told me they watched Unbreakable after seeing and loving Split. And I like The Beast, hell I like everyone in The Horde; especially Barry. Now, I might be Team Glass, but Shout Out to Team Beast. I love you all. THIS WOULDN’T BE HAPPENING WITH OUT YOU!

“Rejoice!” -The Beast

text:
Huh, seen this a few days ago and rolled my eyes and didnt bother to watch. 
Today I was in a movie trailer mood, and Im now glad I decided, "ahh, what the hell".
This is the only trailer so far that Im actually excited about. The only movie that I actually, as of right now, am going to see opening night.
I was also pleasantly surprised to see Bruce, Sam, and Ed reprise their roles. I like superhero movies and I hope this may be the start of something different. A more street level, an everyman kind of supers genre.

I like the Marvel movies, and while I also like DC, they cant seem to decide what they are doing. DC is either overly gritty and then chopped to make it more Marvel. Or, now with SHAZAM! its trying to go, well...I'll just say I wont be watching it.
No matter what, the future of the superhero genre looks promising.
(Still hoping they could do something interesting with Chronicle though)

text:
I had my girlfriend watch my favorite Shyamalan movies (which at the same time are in my top of favorite movies of all time): Unbreakable, Signs, Lady in the Water ... she loved them. A few days later, Split was released. We went to the theater and had a great time, actually split by itself is an excellent movie. When Dunn appeared, we had a mindblow: my girlfriend began to scream of the emotion with her eyes full of tears, I was shocked and unable to speak. 
We couldn't believe what we were seeing. Many who were in the theater but it was obvious  they had not seen Unbreakable didn't understand why so many as my girlfriend, started to get excited and clap hands. Many others like me were petrified totally amazed. It was one of the best moments that I lived in a cinema

It's fun to run, it's fun to play

Ask any woman, child or man

Of broken glass...
Broken Glass....

I like nice girls with gum disease
I like to tickle peoples knees
Don't tell me no just tell me yes

Sometimes I put on special pants
And then I board a plane to France

Like a demon with a side of fries

Of broken glass glass

But you can't eat it
'cause broken glass is not a food
So don't you listen to some dude
Who says put cheese on broken glass
And make a sand-a-wich!
Out of broken glass...

There is no song that can surpass
The song we sing
About broken glass...

text: I have experienced this first hand no joke. I was at this girls house and we smoked way too much. Her boyfriend showed up right as the high was peaking and I lost my mind. I called a friend of mine and I was convinced that she was a psychiatrist trying to calm me down. I explained to her that I couldn't see her and that I was in a room with 2 other people. She asked to talk to one of the other people and I didn't want to let one of the others talk to her but the girl I was with took my phone and started talking to her anyway she tried to explain to her that I just smoked too much. But it felt like she was me like a different personality and that I was in my head looking at these other 2 people and she was lying to the girl on the phone. It was so freaky. Eventually I calmed down and realized I was just really high. I can't imagine how horrible it would be to live with a mental illness that would make every day like this.
author: @transient_at_large

text:
0:58 one of my concerns with the film; the color and lighting compared to Unbreakable. The recreation of the train scene seems to make differences apparent when you compare it to the original (much brighter in new scene and lights look more orange than the original white/blue, scene is lacking the iconically dark blue color). It may be nitpicking, but for me it is what makes it feel less connected to Unbreakable. As for the set of the train, the detail from what we have seen thus far seems to be quite amazing (bag on top left which can be seen in Unbreakable, woman looking for seat with bag on the same shoulder and a coat in hand. Anyone else feel the same from this?
To compare: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5E69-oJ7TDc

text:
Unbreakable and Split are very different from each other but also kinda similar. 

Unbreakable is a slowly paced drama with a satisfying payoff. It's a very interesting take on the superhero genre that I enjoyed greatly. Split is a faster paced horror/thriller that is more intense and satisfying to most audiences. I like both of them very much.

If you haven't seen Unbreakable yet you're not alone, but just know it's not as "have a good time with your friends" friendly as Split. That being said I would still recommend it to all of you.

text:
My theory for the inevitability twist: Mr. Glass has The Horde holding innocent folk hostage, demanding that David Dunn now prove to the world (just as it was proven to Price years ago) that heroes exist or have the deaths of these folks on his head. David fights The Horde anyways, and it looks like Glass will end up killing the innocents himself....

.... suddenly, a shadow shrouded figure appears, breaking Glass and free the hostages, seemingly able to stay one step ahead of The Horde by ducking into shadows, being where the villain isn’t seeing, and striking when he is vulnerable. David shows back up and fully defeats Horde. David then thanks the other guy for helping but tells him he shouldn’t have come alone. The figure says “I’m never alone”... panning back to the other figure, we see the slain folks of The Horde (including the psychologist and the girls who were eaten in Split) disappearing into the shadows around the figure, who is revealed to be Cole Sear from the Sixth Sense (once again played by Haley Joel Osment), who was there to help the ghosts killed by The Horde get their closure (who in turn helped him in the fight by being ghostly eyes and ears to help him evade and attack The Horde).

text:
I haven't had the dream for a while now. 
I used to have it all the time. 
I would be running through the forest. Running away from something, but what ever I was running from It was faster then me. 
Then I would fall forward. 
But before hitting the ground my arms stretch forward, my fingers sink into the earth. 
At that moment I would use my arms and legs to lunge myself forward. As I soar through the air at a remarkable height and speed.  I would land four legged and repeat the process. 
Now the ground between me and my assailant is in my favor. 
Then I wake up and I forget having the dreams. 
Until I see this Trailer, and I realize I should be a writer...

text:
Anyone who is unfamiliar with this 


David was the lone survivor of a fatal train crash that should have definitely killed him. It didn’t which led him to discover he can’t die easily and is only vulnerable in water. But he felt it was his duty to save lives of those around him

Mr. Glass (a comic book writer and a man who is a fragile snowflake) created that trainwreck and multiple disasters to find someone who could survive such tragedies. Thus, finding David. And when he did he believed that ‘he’ himself would be a fit enough super villain for David cause he was his complete opposite. David just called the police on his ass. 

Now splitty Mich split pants has a personality called the ‘beast’ who is able to physically alter their body in order to become almost invincible

And guessing from this trailer Mr glass has found a way to turn these two men against each other creating the perfect comic book hero and villain like in his fantasies and discovers he’s merely the puppet master

text: The twist ending is David Dunn never woke up from the train wreck.  He's been in a coma all this time and Unbreakable and Split all take place in his mind. He wakes at the end of the movie with his family at his side. Mr Glass represents all of the broken bones Dunn suffered in the train wreck and The Beast (and all of the other personalities) represents the fragmentation of Dunn's mind.

text:
Analysis here:
The three colors on the poster correspond the the chakras each character harnesses their strengths from. Purple is mind aka crown, yellow is willpower aka solar plexus. Green is heart. 

They sit in this order to show their relationship with one another. Horde is exhibiting an innocent alter and is not chained; however, David is chained and Glass is "chained" via disability. Put this into perspective, the most dangerous person is smiling, unchained. Both Mr Glass and David disagree on how far to go but both have good intentions through vigilante ways.

It's a good chance Mr Glass and Bruce will work together. They're in psych ward because the only person whose powers are based on belief ie willpower is horde.  The psychologist got Horde to believe he was special rather than discovering it like Glass and David. Without belief Horde is nothing.

text:
It is finally here. The age of post-superhero films. Unbreakable was released in 2000. An incredible film and 20 years before its time. But now we have reached peak superhero cinema where the the masses are teetering on the edge of superhero fatigue and are finally ready. Ready to think more deeply about what superheroes represent as an idea- as a concept- and what they can teach us about ourselves in a profound (and super rad) way.

"Unbreakable" was truly great. "Split" wet our palate and hopefully- hopefully "Glass" will live up to the promise. Bring on the intellectually mature superhero film!

text: Dunn and Glass were the first two superheroes. A duality of eachother. From duality springs, 3 which is macavoys character “the beast”, then 4 which is the girl that survived in split. In Unbreakable David dunn could’ve stopped the beast from becoming a thing by stopping the abusive mother he bumped into at the baseball game. But he didn’t so she went on abusing her son who became the beast. The best then spread this “virus” like propulsion. The girl from the ending of split will now have powers too. These movies  are about “supers” creating eachother. First Glass and Dunn who create the beast, and then the girl from split who the beast created or “infected” in a way. This will continue as each character “spreads” this phenomenon. No doubt this movie will reveal a new super (probably the girl) and create a new one.

It wouldn't be a growing field, based on the case of just one guy.

text: Wow, what an epic trailer. Unbreakable is such an underrated movie. Seeing these 3 characters together makes me so excited. They are all really well developed and complex characters. If you were tired of super hero movies this will surely pull you back in because it's so different, realistic and grounded. Realistic as in; it's not that people can fly or have extreme "superhero" abilities, but more mysteriously, incomprehensible and unexplained powers that just make it more interesting. It's about how these characters interact with each other and the real world. No red capes or million dollar Iron Man expenses. Just a simple raincoat, a wheelchair and a distorted multiple personality disorder. Go watch this movie. But before you do, watch Unbreakable (2000) first, then (Split 2016). You will not be disapointed. Buy a ticket and go see this movie. *Thumbs up* and thanks for reading.

text: Keep an eye on who you think the protagonist is. I don't think it's necessarily going to be Dunn vs. Price and Crumb. Dr. Staple may serve as a primary protagonist or antagonist in this - she represents the world who does not accept the idea of supernatural heroes, an idea which Elijah is committed to spreading across the world. In this regard, Dunn is her enemy just as much as the other two. Is the true conflict of the movie going to be Elijah pushing comic tropes upon the world? Remember, Dunn defeated that in Unbreakable - he could have had a 'superhero confrontation' with Glass at the end but instead did the right thing and turned him in to the authorities in an anti-climactic fashion.

text:
Completely UNRELATED comment ahead! 
If you don't read you WILL be abducted by grey aliens tonight 👽 (no pressure) When life gets rough there's always that one thing we as humans can go back to and feel like everything is alright. In my opinion that thing is good music. There have been so many days before when I was younger where everything seemed so overwhelmingly negative... Like there was no light at the end of my tunnel. But then I started listening to my idols, marshmello and zedd included, and somehow it made me feel some sort of way... Almost like a hope entered into my life. At that moment I knew what my dream in life was.

 I wanted to make music like my favorite artists. I knew I'd never be able to even afford a keyboard so it stayed a dream, buried deep inside me never to see the light of day. Until now that is. Here I am  few years later and I'm making the music I'd always look for but never could find. This journey in my life has been amazing... And I want to help people feel the same way by listening to the music I create... I wanna be able to make memories for other people. I want people to hear my music and let it change your day for the better! That's why I started making music. 🌌They're not your typical dance tracks. My songs are flooded with emotion and vibes.

 They're very spacey and futuristic. They make you feel like your life is part of something bigger, almost like your being whisked away to another galaxy... I'm not trying to boast. I'm just trying to help you all feel good vibes. And in 2018, I think alot more people need that. If you could help me out by checking my songs it would mean so much. I promise you won't regret it. Thank you for being one of my first fans if you choose to listen. 🙏 I'll forever remember the first people who supported me... <3

text: I am a comic book AND a film fanatic.  I am an M. Night fan.  I can't wait for this.  As much as Dark Knight changed comic book movies, Unbreakable was the first to prove the seriousness of Comic related movies.  I have faith in this.  I actually enjoyed the Avatar movie, but I didn't follow the show.  I don't know what spin M, Night can throw our way.  I don't care if there is one.  Unbreakable showed what a comic book movie could be if done correctly in a mature manner.  It was a superb hero origin.  Split, was a superb villain origin.  Glass,  I just was a good comic movie bringing them together.

text: I think M. Night's message in this movie is going to be that "Belief" is strong enough to make something reality. These three people don't really have super powers, but their belief that they do is what makes it real. This is a very profound concept, one of Mind over Matter. The only flaw with this theory is that in Unbreakable David(Bruce Willis) never realized he was invincible until after Mr. Glass found him and planted the seed of thought into his mind. But I'm sure this could be corrected with a few lines of dialogue. Regardless, I'm very excited to see what M. Night gives us, because I'm a huge fan of the Unbreakable universe, and this trailer is a dream come true for me.

text:
If this is a cinematic universe, the battle between the three guys here should spill into the Village, revealing the truth to it's inhabitants. Mel Gibson is institutionalized because he claims he fought demons(If you guys had paid attention while watching Signs, you'd know that they are demons, not aliens). Walberg is also curious about Dun, Glass and Kevin and wonder if their abilities are connected to the plants, we also learn that the Lady in the Water also a connection to wherever all these powers are coming from. We also learn that the institution the 3 guys are kept at is where Nana and Pop Pop were held before they escaped. 

In a later movie, a new heroine called Korra enters the frey who ponders if what has been happening to all the others guys, is what's allowing her to manipulate the 4 elements. She later recieves vision from a mysterious being of Light called Raava, as well as two other strange fish like beings who claim to be the moon and ocean. All three of the tell her to seek out the other heroes. The mysterious force that is causing all this crazy stuff to happen is also causing animals to become wilder and more ferocious, and if that's not enough, Osmond ponders why Dun looks like a ghost teacher he had as a child. Also, for the cherry on the top, all the heroes come face to face with a strange guy who seems to always pop up all the time. One time he appeared as Osmond's doctor, he later was Gibson's neighbor, later as a security guard at a national park, and most recently he was the landlord of Macavoy's psychiatrist. Turns out he's some sort of magic advisor who will sometimes appear to give advice, wisdom or help people out with their problems. If all the heroes then unite to stop a new yet to be revealed villain, the strange guy then says "My work is done." and vanishes.

M. Night: You know what the scariest thing is? To not know your place in this world, to not know why you're here. That's... that's just an awful feeling.

Split: What have you done?

Mrs. Price: Mm-hmm. That's what I said to my son. But he says there's always two kinds; there's the soldier villain — who fights the hero with his hands; and then there's the real threat — the brilliant and evil archenemy — who fights the hero with his mind.
